Package de.uka.ilkd.key.gui.smt

Class DropdownSelectionButton

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.gui.smt.DropdownSelectionButton
public class DropdownSelectionButton extends Object
A button consisting of an action component (normal button) and a selection component (dropdown button). The selection button opens a dropdown menu that lets the user select some previously added actions and combines those selected actions into a resulting one according to some reducing function (reduceChoice). The resulting action is executed when pressing the action button. The selection button's dropdown menu can also contain other components (addComponent(Component)).

    setEmptyItem(String text, String toolTip)
    Set the information for the empty item.
    void
    setEnabled(boolean b)
    Enables or disables the action button (enabling is only possible if the items aren't empty).
    void
    setItems(Action[] it, Function<Action[],Action> reduce, int maxChoice)
    Set the actions that can be selected, the maximum amount of items that can be selected at the same time (a positive integer) and the function used to create the action component's action out of all the selected actions.
    void
    setPrefix(String prefix)
    Set the prefix String that will be prepended to all items in the menu.
    void
    setSelectedItem(Action item)
    Set the executedAction to be the given one and update the action button.

  • Constructor Details

    • DropdownSelectionButton

      public DropdownSelectionButton(int iconSize)
      Create a new DropdownSelectionButton with a given icon size used to display the selection component's icon.
      Parameters:
      iconSize - the size of the selection component's icon (e.g. down-arrow)

  • Method Details

    • setEnabled

      public void setEnabled(boolean b)
      Enables or disables the action button (enabling is only possible if the items aren't empty).
      Parameters:
      b - true iff the action button should be enabled

    • addListener

      public void addListener(ChangeListener listener)
      Add a ChangeListener to this button that will be notified by #setSelectedItem(). A listener cannot be added more than once (nothing will change if it is already present).
      Parameters:
      listener - the new ChangeListener

    • removeListener

      public void removeListener(ChangeListener listener)
      Remove a previously added ChangeListener from this button.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to remove

    • getSelectionComponent

      public JComponent getSelectionComponent()
      Returns:
      the selection button (opens the selection dropdown menu)

    • getActionComponent

      public JComponent getActionComponent()
      Returns:
      the action button (runs the selected action)

    • setAction

      public void setAction(Action action)
      Set the action to be executed by the action component.
      Parameters:
      action - the action to be executed

    • getAction

      public Action getAction()
      Returns:
      the action that is executed by the action component

    • getSelectedItems

      public Action[] getSelectedItems()
      Returns:
      the currently selected actions in the selection component

    • isEmptyItem

      public boolean isEmptyItem()
      Returns:
      whether the currently executed action is the empty item set by #setEmptyItem()

    • getEmptyItem

      public Action getEmptyItem()
      Returns:
      the currently set empty item

    • setEmptyItem

      public void setEmptyItem(String text, String toolTip)
      Set the information for the empty item.
      Parameters:
      text - the text displayed in the action button when the empty item is executed
      toolTip - the tooltip of the empty item

    • setPrefix

      public void setPrefix(String prefix)
      Set the prefix String that will be prepended to all items in the menu.
      Parameters:
      prefix - the new prefix

    • contains

      public boolean contains(Action item)
      Check whether a given action is contained in the actions that can be selected via the selection component.
      Parameters:
      item - the action to search
      Returns:
      whether the given item can be selected

    • setSelectedItem

      public void setSelectedItem(Action item)
      Set the executedAction to be the given one and update the action button. Notify the ChangeListeners of this change.
      Parameters:
      item - the new executedAction

    • getSelectionButton

      protected JButton getSelectionButton()
      (Create and) return the selection button used by #getSelectionComponent().
      Returns:
      the selection button

    • getActionButton

      protected JButton getActionButton()
      (Create and) return the action button used by #getActionComponent().
      Returns:
      the action button

    • getMenu

      protected JPopupMenu getMenu()
      (Create and) return the dropdown popup menu that is opened by the selection component. Deletes all added components when the menu is null, thus leading a completely fresh and empty menu.
      Returns:
      the popup menu opened by the selection button

    • setItems

      public void setItems(Action[] it, Function<Action[],Action> reduce, int maxChoice)
      Set the actions that can be selected, the maximum amount of items that can be selected at the same time (a positive integer) and the function used to create the action component's action out of all the selected actions.
      Parameters:
      it - the selectable actions
      reduce - the function used to collapse multiple selected actions into one for the action component
      maxChoice - the maximum amount of actions that can be selected, this is assumed to be at least 1 (otherwise it is changed to be 1)

    • refreshSelectionItems

      public void refreshSelectionItems(Collection<JMenuItem> newMenuItems)
      Set new selection items/actions while keeping all other components of the popup menu.
      Parameters:
      newMenuItems - the new actions that can be selected

    • addComponent

      public void addComponent(Component comp)
      Add a component to the popup menu (below the selection items).
      Parameters:
      comp - the added component

    • removeComponent

      public void removeComponent(Component comp)
      Remove a component (other than the selection items) from the popup menu.
      Parameters:
      comp - the component to remove

    • getTopItem

      public Action getTopItem()
      Returns:
      the first action of the items list or the empty item if items is empty.

    • selectMaxNumber

      public void selectMaxNumber()
      Select the first maxChoiceAmount items of the items list.

    • deselectAll

      public void deselectAll()
      Deselect all currently selected items.
